title: Mildly Briny British Lamb Pie And Mash
description: A traditional British dish featuring lamb, raisins, and creamy mash, with a mildly briny flavor, this satvik-friendly meal is perfect for a comforting dinner
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the melted score-fat over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrot and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the raisins, flour, vegetable broth, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a 9 inch pie dish.
In a large pot, boil the chopped potato until tender, about 15 minutes.
Drain the potato and mash with the milk until smooth and creamy.
Spread the mashed potato over the lamb mixture in the pie dish.
Bake the pie in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the potato is golden brown.
